req.title: Overdraft protection
req.statement: An overdraft shall trigger a draw from the linked savings account up to the linked account's available balance; otherwise the transaction shall be declined.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-012 defines three distinct decision branches: (1) overdraft succeeds when linked savings has sufficient funds, (2) overdraft fails when linked savings balance is insufficient, and (3) boundary where linked savings balance exactly equals the overdraft shortfall. EP covers the normal success path; NEG covers the decline path; BVA probes the exact-balance edge; STC traces the account state change from normal to overdrawn; DT captures the combined condition matrix. Each test resets account state via fixtures to guarantee idempotency.

tests.name: test_overdraft_succeeds_when_savings_has_sufficient_balance
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking balance $50.00
Savings account linked with available balance $200.00
Overdraft protection is enabled for the customer
actions: POST /overdraft with debit amount $120.00 against checking account
expected: Savings account debited by $70.00 (shortfall) plus applicable fee
Overdraft fee assessed and present in response
trace: R-FIN-012: debit exceeds checking balance trigger
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"

@pytest.fixture
def overdraft_sufficient_state():
    account = <fixture: create_account>(checking=50_00, savings=200_00, overdraft_linked=True)
    yield account
    <helper_fn: teardown_account>(account["customer_id"])

def test_overdraft_succeeds_when_savings_has_sufficient_balance(overdraft_sufficient_state):
    acct = overdraft_sufficient_state
    payload = {
        "customer_id": acct["customer_id"],
        "checking_account_id": acct["checking_id"],
        "amount": 120_00
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["approved"] is True
    assert body["overdraft_fee"] > 0
    savings = <helper_fn: get_balance>(acct["savings_id"])
    shortfall = 120_00 - 50_00
    assert savings <= 200_00 - shortfall

tests.name: test_overdraft_declined_when_savings_balance_insufficient
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking balance $10.00
Savings account linked with available balance $30.00
Overdraft protection is enabled for the customer
actions: POST /overdraft with debit amount $100.00 against checking account
expected: Decline reason indicates insufficient linked balance
No debit applied to savings account
trace: R-FIN-012: declined when linked balance insufficient
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"

@pytest.fixture
def overdraft_insufficient_state():
    account = <fixture: create_account>(checking=10_00, savings=30_00, overdraft_linked=True)
    yield account
    <helper_fn: teardown_account>(account["customer_id"])

def test_overdraft_declined_when_savings_balance_insufficient(overdraft_insufficient_state):
    acct = overdraft_insufficient_state
    payload = {
        "customer_id": acct["customer_id"],
        "checking_account_id": acct["checking_id"],
        "amount": 100_00
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (402, 422)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["approved"] is False
    assert "insufficient" in body.get("decline_reason", "").lower()
    savings_after = <helper_fn: get_balance>(acct["savings_id"])
    assert savings_after == 30_00

tests.name: test_overdraft_boundary_savings_exactly_covers_shortfall
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking balance $50.00
Savings account linked with available balance exactly $70.00 (exact shortfall)
Overdraft protection is enabled for the customer
actions: POST /overdraft with debit amount $120.00 against checking account
expected: Savings account balance reduced to $0.00 after shortfall draw
Overdraft fee assessed and present in response
trace: R-FIN-012: linked savings debited up to available balance
R-FIN-012: boundary — savings balance equals exact shortfall
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"

@pytest.fixture
def overdraft_exact_boundary_state():
    account = <fixture: create_account>(checking=50_00, savings=70_00, overdraft_linked=True)
    yield account
    <helper_fn: teardown_account>(account["customer_id"])

def test_overdraft_boundary_savings_exactly_covers_shortfall(overdraft_exact_boundary_state):
    acct = overdraft_exact_boundary_state
    payload = {
        "customer_id": acct["customer_id"],
        "checking_account_id": acct["checking_id"],
        "amount": 120_00
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["approved"] is True
    assert body["overdraft_fee"] > 0
    savings_after = <helper_fn: get_balance>(acct["savings_id"])
    assert savings_after == 0

tests.name: test_overdraft_state_transition_normal_to_overdrawn
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking balance $100.00
Savings account linked with available balance $500.00
Account state is NORMAL (no prior overdraft)
actions: POST /overdraft with debit amount $150.00 to trigger overdraft
Verify account state transitions from NORMAL to OVERDRAWN then back to NORMAL after draw
expected: Post-transaction account state reflects overdraft draw applied
trace: R-FIN-012: state transition — checking goes from sufficient to overdrawn, savings absorbs shortfall
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"

@pytest.fixture
def overdraft_state_transition_acct():
    account = <fixture: create_account>(checking=100_00, savings=500_00, overdraft_linked=True)
    yield account
    <helper_fn: teardown_account>(account["customer_id"])

def test_overdraft_state_transition_normal_to_overdrawn(overdraft_state_transition_acct):
    acct = overdraft_state_transition_acct
    pre_state = <helper_fn: get_account_state>(acct["checking_id"])
    assert pre_state == "NORMAL"
    payload = {
        "customer_id": acct["customer_id"],
        "checking_account_id": acct["checking_id"],
        "amount": 150_00
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["approved"] is True
    assert body["overdraft_fee"] > 0
    savings_after = <helper_fn: get_balance>(acct["savings_id"])
    assert savings_after == 500_00 - (150_00 - 100_00)

tests.name: test_overdraft_decision_table_no_linked_account
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking balance $20.00
No savings account linked to checking account
Overdraft protection is disabled or no linkage exists
actions: POST /overdraft with debit amount $80.00 against checking account with no linked savings
expected: Decline reason references missing or unlinked savings account
trace: R-FIN-012: precondition — overdraft linked; absence of linkage is a DT false-branch
R-FIN-012: error pathway — declined when linked balance insufficient (no account = zero balance)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"

@pytest.fixture
def no_linked_savings_acct():
    account = <fixture: create_account>(checking=20_00, savings=None, overdraft_linked=False)
    yield account
    <helper_fn: teardown_account>(account["customer_id"])

def test_overdraft_decision_table_no_linked_account(no_linked_savings_acct):
    acct = no_linked_savings_acct
    payload = {
        "customer_id": acct["customer_id"],
        "checking_account_id": acct["checking_id"],
        "amount": 80_00
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (400, 422)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["approved"] is False
    checking_after = <helper_fn: get_balance>(acct["checking_id"])
    assert checking_after == 20_00
